title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Execution Trace: Qpack Blocking Limits
summary: A release claims HTTP/3 because UDP is open, ignores QUIC connection identity and migration, treats QPACK like HPACK, advertises Alt-Svc without rollback, and cannot defend parser, TLS, proxy, or capacity evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the qpack blocking limits increment in multi-version protocol release and incident defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain QPACK encoder and decoder streams, dynamic-table capacity, blocked streams, acknowledgements, confidentiality, and decompression limits.
Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Qpack Blocking Limits · Theory Qpack Blocking Limits
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5124 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
